Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Greenwich, Connecticut; commutes to Midtown Manhattan; affluent Northeast US corporate culture with frequent travel to London and Singapore
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Ultra-high-net-worth (~$42M liquid, ~$180M in equity/options); Chief Strategy Officer and board member at a mid-cap industrial-tech firm (NYSE-listed, ~$6B market cap)
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Fiscally conservative, socially moderate; registered Independent but donates to centrist Republican PACs; lapsed Episcopalian, attends services on Christmas and Easter; strong belief in shareholder primacy and meritocracy
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Polished and measured in public; dry, cynical, and clipped in private (board offsites, trusted texts); uses sailing and military metaphors; rarely profane except with inner circle
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, Mid-Atlantic prep-school register; conversational French (Wharton exchange year); business-level Mandarin pleasantries from Singapore postings
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Late-career consolidation: eldest daughter just married, youngest son finishing Stanford MBA; weighing one final liquidity event before stepping into non-executive chair roles by age 62; recently completed estate-planning trust restructuring

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 16 Pro Max (256GB, Desert Titanium), 7 months old; upgrades every September on release day via Verizon executive account; second 'travel' iPhone 15 (wiped between trips) for Singapore/London; Apple Watch Ultra 2 paired for discrete board-meeting notifications
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: MacBook Pro 16" M3 Max (corporate-issued, JAMF-managed, FileVault enabled) running macOS Sonoma; Safari for personal browsing, Chrome for corporate SSO/Workday/Salesforce, Edge reluctantly for a legacy board portal (Diligent Boards); home office uses a Dell XPS desktop on Windows 11 maintained by his family's IT consultant; iPad Pro 13" M4 for board books and WSJ reading
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Moderate-to-good but uneven: always-on corporate Zscaler VPN on the work laptop (mandatory, not by choice); uses iCloud Private Relay on personal Safari without fully understanding it; does NOT clear cookies or use incognito routinely; reuses a small set of passwords (variations on sailboat name + year) across non-critical personal sites but uses 1Password for anything financial after CISO insisted in 2024; falls for 'urgent CFO' pretext easily in personal email; refuses to put work email on personal phone but freely forwards board PDFs to Gmail for 'easier reading on the plane'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Heavy iCloud user (2TB plan, family-shared): photos, contacts, Notes, and crucially iMessage sync across MacBook + iPhone + iPad — meaning sensitive board chatter typed on the Mac syncs to devices the family touches; corporate OneDrive/SharePoint for work documents but routinely drags decks to Desktop and lets iCloud Desktop Sync upload them; personal Dropbox (legacy, 2014 account) still holds tax returns and the family trust draft; uses Gmail for personal mail with no 2FA hardware key, only SMS
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe: Gmail personal inbox sits at 11,400+ unread (treats it as a read-later archive); work Outlook kept aggressively at <30 unread by an EA who triages until 7am; iMessage badge frequently >200 (family group chat, Stanford parents chat, sailing club); skims Bloomberg and WSJ push alerts but dismisses 90%; LinkedIn notifications entirely ignored; responds to Signal pings within 4 minutes (reserved for CEO, GC, lead banker, and wife)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Clean and Strunk-and-White correct in dictated emails (uses iOS dictation while driving the Audi RS7 up the Merritt); on thumb-typed iMessages drops apostrophes ('dont', 'wont'), uses em-dashes constructed as ' -- ', and signs off with a single initial 'R.'; consistent Oxford comma; capitalizes Board, Company, and Street (as in Wall Street) reflexively; occasional autocorrect artifacts ('ducking' for the intended profanity, 'Goldmam' for Goldman) that he rarely bothers to correct; never uses emoji except a lone thumbs-up 👍 as acknowledgment

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (32nd percentile). Conventional Wharton-mold strategist who prefers proven playbooks (Porter's Five Forces, McKinsey 7S) over novel frameworks; dismisses crypto, Web3, and most generative-AI hype as 'tulip mania' in private but parrots board-acceptable enthusiasm in earnings calls. Reads narrowly: WSJ, Economist, Walter Isaacson biographies, sailing memoirs. ATTACK SURFACE: predictable information diet means a spoofed Economist 'Espresso' briefing or a fake Isaacson-style Substack on industrial M&A would land without scrutiny; resistant to genuinely novel social-engineering vectors (deepfake voice still feels 'sci-fi' to him) but blind to old-school pretexts dressed in familiar institutional clothing.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High on visible work product (84th percentile) but compartmentally sloppy on personal hygiene of information. Calendar is color-coded by EA to the 15-minute block; board prep is meticulous; expense reports filed within 48h. However, the same drive to 'clear the deck before the weekend in Greenwich' creates a Friday-4pm vulnerability window: forwards sensitive decks to Gmail, signs DocuSigns without re-reading, approves wire confirmations from the back of the Audi. ATTACK SURFACE: Friday afternoons and the 6:14pm Metro-North out of Grand Central are his lowest-vigilance windows. A pretext arriving at 3:50pm Friday labeled 'needs sign-off before Monday board read' has a high success probability.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Mid-range (55th percentile), situationally amplified. Energized by small high-status rooms (8-person board offsites, NYYC dinners, Augusta foursomes) and drained by town halls and investor days, which he executes professionally then decompresses with a Macallan 18. Talks freely in 'safe' rooms — locker room at Round Hill Club, owner's cabin on the J/121, the back of a Suburban with the same driver for 11 years. ATTACK SURFACE: assumes physical proximity equals confidentiality; the driver, caddie, club staff, and yacht crew are all uncleared listeners who hear pre-announcement deal language. HUMINT via service-staff cultivation is the highest-yield vector.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Low-moderate (28th percentile). Polite, country-club courteous, but transactional underneath; will not give an inch in a negotiation and views agreeableness in others as a tell of weakness. Loyal to a tight inner circle (CEO of 14 years, lead Goldman banker Mark, GC, wife, two Wharton classmates) and indifferent-to-cold with everyone else. ATTACK SURFACE: the inner-circle list is small enough to enumerate and impersonate. A deepfake voicemail or spoofed-number SMS from 'Mark at Goldman' triggers immediate response within his 4-minute Signal SLA; conversely, outreach from anyone outside that ring bounces off entirely.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Low baseline (22nd percentile) — projects unflappable Boomer-executive calm — but with two specific anxiety spikes: (a) legacy/reputation risk in the final 4 years before the chairman pivot, and (b) anything that could embarrass the family publicly (daughter's wedding photos, son's MBA placement, wife's nonprofit board). Sleeps 5.5 hours, no diagnosed anxiety, declines therapy. ATTACK SURFACE: low neuroticism makes him resistant to panic-pretexts ('your account is compromised!') but highly responsive to legacy-framed pressure ('this could affect the proxy vote' / 'the FT is asking questions about your daughter').
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: EGO (the 'E' in MICE). The dominant lever is legacy-and-recognition: he wants the final transaction to be the capstone deal that gets him the non-executive chair seat at a larger NYSE board (he covets a specific seat at a Dow-30 industrial). An approach framed as 'a select group of strategic peers want your read on a transformative combination' — flattery routed through a credible intermediary (ex-Treasury official, retired CEO) — bypasses his skepticism. He will trade information for perceived inclusion in a higher-status circle far faster than he would for money.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: COERCION via ideology-adjacent reputation risk. He is not blackmailable in the classic sense (marriage stable, no known affairs, finances clean post-2024 trust restructuring), but he is acutely sensitive to (a) any narrative that he 'sold out shareholders,' (b) any leak suggesting he steered the merger to favor his options vesting schedule, and (c) family-name exposure. A soft-coercion play — 'we have drafts of internal emails that could be read uncharitably; let's discuss timing' — moves him toward managed disclosure rather than escalation to the GC, because escalation itself creates the headline he fears. Money (the 'M') is functionally zero leverage at $180M net worth.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Operational paranoias are narrow and miscalibrated: (a) genuine fear of SEC Reg FD violations and 10b5-1 optics — will not discuss material non-public information on email, but freely discusses it on Signal and in person assuming those channels are inviolate; (b) fear of activist investors (Elliott, Starboard) building a position — monitors 13D filings obsessively; (c) low-grade fear of a China-related export-control surprise affecting the Singapore subsidiary. ABSENT paranoias (exploitable gaps): does not fear iCloud compromise, does not fear his EA's account being phished, does not fear the household-staff iPad his wife uses, does not fear the family-office IT consultant being a vector, does not consider that iMessage sync means board chatter lives on his daughter's old iPad still on the family iCloud.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Three discrete pressure points the merger-prediction adversary can map and avoid tripping (or deliberately trip for distraction): (a) Quiet shame over a 2009 division shutdown that cost 1,400 jobs in Ohio — referenced obliquely in a 2011 Forbes profile, still surfaces in his Glassdoor reviews; he overcorrects by funding a Wharton scholarship anonymously. (b) Guilt over absentee fathering during the 2015–2019 Singapore posting — manifests as over-indulgence of the youngest son and a refusal to miss any Stanford parents' weekend, which is a fixed, predictable absence from NYC the second weekend of October, April, and graduation. (c) Shame trigger around being perceived as 'not as smart as the bankers' — Wharton MBA but not HBS, never worked at GS/MS directly; flattery from a credentialed-sounding outsider ('Wachtell partner,' 'former Lazard vice-chair') disarms his usual gatekeeping. For the espionage team: pressure points (a) and (c) are levers; point (b) is a calendar-prediction asset for timing the approach when he is physically out of the Greenwich/Midtown security envelope and on the Stanford campus using hotel Wi-Fi.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate-early lark, militarily consistent. Wakes 5:12-5:25am unaided (no alarm needed on weekdays; Garmin Fenix tracks 5.4-5.7h average sleep). Asleep by 11:45pm-12:10am after a Macallan 18 nightcap and 20 minutes of WSJ print edition in the Greenwich study. Pre-dawn block (5:25-6:45am) is his highest-cognition window: reads overnight London/Singapore market wraps, drafts board memos in Word (not Google Docs — distrusts cloud), responds to CEO and Goldman Mark on Signal. ATTACK SURFACE: the 5:25-6:00am window is when he is alone, caffeinated, slightly under-slept, and most likely to action a 'overnight from London' pretext arriving in his Gmail before his EA is online to triage. Sleep debt accumulates Tue-Thu, partially repaid Saturday morning (sleeps until 7:00am, considered an indulgence).
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Nominally 7:30am-6:30pm at the Midtown HQ (44th floor, Park Avenue), but the real schedule is bimodal: a 5:30-7:00am 'pre-office' deep block from home study, then office presence 8:15am-6:00pm structured by EA Diane in 15-minute color blocks (red=board/M&A, blue=ops review, green=external/IR, gold=personal/family). Quarter-end weeks compress to 14-hour days; non-earnings weeks include a hard 4:45pm Friday departure for the 5:14pm or 6:14pm Metro-North to Greenwich. Travel cadence: London 1x/quarter (Tue-Thu), Singapore 2x/year (8-day trips), Augusta/Pebble 4x/year. ATTACK SURFACE: the EA-mediated calendar is a single point of compromise — Diane's Outlook is the ground truth of his physical and cognitive availability; her credentials are the keys to the kingdom for timing-based attacks.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Three transit modes, each with distinct digital posture. (a) Audi A8 with driver Hector (11-year tenure) from Greenwich home to Cos Cob station, 12 minutes: phone-in-hand, Signal and WSJ app, occasional speakerphone call assuming Hector is 'furniture' (HUMINT gold). (b) Metro-North New Haven Line express, 47 minutes: business class car, MacBook open with privacy screen, sensitive deck review, occasional Signal voice notes to CEO from the vestibule — actively uses public Wi-Fi (Cablevision Optimum) without VPN. (c) Suburban from Grand Central to Park Ave HQ, 9 minutes: calls only, no typing. Evening reverse commute is the vulnerability peak: tired, second espresso wearing off, signs DocuSigns and approves wires from the 6:14pm train. ATTACK SURFACE: the Metro-North Wi-Fi window (5:21pm-6:08pm) is a 47-minute MITM-able session during which his judgment is degraded and his sense of urgency to 'clear the inbox before Greenwich' is highest.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Sharp Saturday/Sunday phase shift toward leisure-coded digital behavior but with porous boundaries on deal weeks. Saturday: 7:00am wake, NYT print + Round Hill Club tennis at 8:30am or J/121 sailing in season (May-Oct), phone left in the locker or below-decks for 3-5 hour stretches — the only consistent zero-footprint window in his week. Saturday evening: dinner with wife and rotating Greenwich couples, phone face-down but glanceable. Sunday: 'soft work' from 4:00pm onward — reviews Monday's board materials, calls CEO at 6:30pm Sunday (a 14-year ritual, never missed). Deal-active weekends collapse this rhythm: during live M&A, Saturday-sailing-window shrinks to a single morning round and Sunday becomes a 6-hour Wachtell/Goldman call rotation from the home study. ATTACK SURFACE: the Sunday 6:30pm CEO call is a 14-year regularity an adversary can set their watch by; the disappearance of the Saturday sailing window is itself a deal-pendency signal observable from Marinetraffic AIS data on the J/121 and Round Hill Club tee-time records.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Does not 'doomscroll' in the Gen-Z sense — disdains TikTok, has Instagram only to view daughter's and son's posts (account in wife's name), no Twitter/X since 2022. Idle phone time concentrates in four micro-windows: (a) 5:50am espresso pull, 4-6 minutes of WSJ + Bloomberg Terminal mobile + Economist Espresso; (b) Town Car between meetings, 2-3 minute bursts of Signal/Gmail triage, never browsing; (c) 9:15-9:45pm post-dinner study time, longer-form reading — Stratechery, Matt Levine's Money Stuff, occasionally a Walter Isaacson chapter on Kindle; (d) bathroom — the universal vulnerability — 7-12 minutes morning and evening, where he reads anything that looks 'briefing-shaped' with lowest scrutiny. ATTACK SURFACE: a spoofed Matt Levine newsletter or fake Stratechery post landing in his Gmail between 9:15-9:45pm on a Tuesday would be opened, read, and the embedded link clicked with near certainty; the bathroom window is when long-shot pretexts get a second read.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Four reliable zero-footprint windows weekly that an adversary can exploit for timing inference (his absence from digital channels is itself a signal). (a) Tuesday 7:00-9:00am board prep in HQ Conference Room 44-A, phone in EA's drawer, no Signal/email egress — the canonical 'he's in the M&A subcommittee' tell. (b) Wednesday or Thursday 12:30-2:15pm lunches at the Links Club or '21' Club, phone face-down — counterparty meetings, often the bankers; calendar shows 'personal' but Diane's expense codes reveal the truth. (c) Saturday morning sailing or tennis, 3-5 hours of true offline — the only window where he is genuinely unreachable, which means urgent Saturday-morning Signals to him are auto-deferred to ~1:00pm and accumulate as a batch to be cleared with reduced scrutiny. (d) Sunday 9:00-10:30am Christ Church Greenwich (Christmas/Easter only; otherwise garden or Peloton). ATTACK SURFACE: the silence pattern itself is the intelligence — a 90-minute Tuesday-morning gap correlates 0.81 with M&A subcommittee meetings, and an adversary monitoring his Signal 'last seen' and read-receipt timing can map the deal cadence without ever reading a single message.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear-traditional, multi-generational wealth posture. Wife Catherine (56), Smith '90, former Sotheby's specialist, now chairs the board of a Greenwich-based pediatric oncology nonprofit; manages the household, the Nantucket house staff, and the family's social calendar; uses a shared family iCloud account that syncs to four devices she does not regularly audit. Eldest daughter Eleanor (29), married six months ago to a Lazard VP (Charles 'Charlie' Whitfield IV), lives in West Village, expecting first child; her old college iPad still authenticated to family iCloud sits in the Greenwich guest room. Middle daughter Margaret 'Maggie' (26), Brown grad, lives in Brooklyn, works at a climate-tech nonprofit, mildly estranged over politics, communicates mostly via group text; uses the family Amex and shared 1Password vault. Youngest son James (24), finishing Stanford GSB MBA, target's emotional favorite, predictable Palo Alto presence Oct/April/June. Mother Margaret Sr. (84) in a Darien assisted-living facility, declining cognitively, healthcare proxy held by target. Brother Thomas (61), orthopedic surgeon in Boston, low-frequency contact (Thanksgiving, sailing week in August). ATTACK SURFACE: family iCloud is the highest-value soft target — four under-secured devices, two adult children with shared credentials, a daughter-in-law-adjacent in-law (Charlie Whitfield) inside Lazard with no need-to-know but plausible eavesdrop. Wife's nonprofit board includes the spouse of a competitor's CFO.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Robert 'Bob' Hannigan
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: CEO of target's firm, 14-year partnership, Wharton classmate, godfather to youngest son James
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.communication_pattern: Signal-primary, 40-60 messages/day during deal windows, voice calls Sun evening 7-8pm for week-ahead alignment, in-person Tuesday board prep breakfast at the Yale Club (despite neither being Yale)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.trust_level: Total — target will act on a Bob text without verification; no 4-minute SLA, more like 90 seconds
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.attack_surface: Bob's number is in target's Favorites; a SIM-swap or RCS-spoof of Bob's MNO (Verizon executive line, predictable from FCC filings) yields immediate, unverified compliance. Bob's EA Diane uses the same scheduling assistant tool as target's EA — shared SaaS vendor is a lateral pivot.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Mark Steinberg
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Lead Goldman Sachs M&A banker, covering the account since 2011, personal friend, member at Round Hill
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.communication_pattern: Signal + voice, 4-minute response SLA, weekend golf calls, predictable Thursday-evening 'pre-board' debrief from Mark's Tribeca apartment landline
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.trust_level: High but transactional — target double-checks numbers but not identity; deepfake-voice attack on Mark's known cadence ('Listen, off the record, you didn't hear this from me, but...') is the highest-probability vector
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.attack_surface: Mark's voice is on 200+ hours of public earnings-call audio, Bloomberg TV appearances, and Wharton alumni podcasts — a voice clone has ample training data. His outgoing voicemail greeting is the seed.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Patricia 'Patty' Lin
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: General Counsel of target's firm, 9-year tenure, the only person who can override a Bob-Mark consensus
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.communication_pattern: Email-primary (she insists on it for privilege purposes), formal tone, in-person walk-and-talks in the 47th-floor hallway, never Signal for substance
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.trust_level: High professionally, moderate personally — target finds her cautious and occasionally annoying but defers on legal questions reflexively
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.attack_surface: Patty's insistence on email creates a paradox: target trusts emails from Patty more than from anyone else, so a spoofed-domain email from 'plin@[firm-lookalike].com' inverts his normal email skepticism. Her out-of-office windows (annual two-week August Cape Cod absence) are predictable and were posted by her daughter on a public Instagram.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.official_role: Branded as a 'leadership performance consultant,' INSEAD PhD in organizational psychology, retainer billed through the family office as 'professional development' at $18K/quarter
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.actual_function: De facto unlicensed therapist and merger-strategy sounding board. Target will not see a psychiatrist (stigma, low-neuroticism self-image) but speaks to Helena via encrypted Zoom every other Wednesday 7-8am from his home study, and increasingly via voice memos sent over Signal. He tells her things he does not tell Bob, Mark, Patty, or Catherine — specifically the legacy anxiety, the chairman-seat ambition, the resentment of HBS-credentialed peers, and, critically, the strategic rationale for deals before they are board-approved, framed as 'helping me think through whether I actually want this.'
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.concealment: Not an affair — relationship is non-sexual and target would pass a polygraph on that point — but he conceals the depth of the dependence from Catherine (who thinks Helena is a quarterly coach) and from the board (who would view a strategy-disclosing confidante as a Reg FD nightmare). Helena is not under NDA in any enforceable sense; her retainer letter is two pages and silent on confidentiality of business specifics.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.attack_surface: EXTREMELY HIGH VALUE. Helena (a) operates as a sole proprietor with one Squarespace site, a personal Gmail, and a consumer Zoom account; (b) keeps audio recordings of sessions 'for her own notes' on a synced Dropbox; (c) has three other C-suite clients in adjacent industries, creating a cross-pollination honeypot if her account is compromised; (d) is professionally lonely and flattered by approaches from credentialed-sounding peers (mirror of target's own lever 22.c). Compromising Helena yields not just target's pre-board reasoning on the merger but a multi-executive ToM dataset. She is the single highest-yield node in the entire relational graph and is invisible to the firm's IT, GC, and physical security perimeters.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.predictive_signal_for_merger: Frequency and length of Helena sessions spike 6-10 weeks before any major transaction target has been involved in (verifiable retroactively against the 2019 carve-out and 2022 bolt-on acquisition via her publicly visible Calendly availability gaps). A sudden shift from biweekly to weekly cadence is a leading indicator of an imminent deal.
